title: NATS connect with tls.rejectUnauthorized: false — the NATS server certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The NATS client validates the server certificate (tls.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: NATS connect is configured with tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the NATS server's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — reading and injecting messages on every subject (events, commands, credentials). Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the server CA via tls.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No NATS connect call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: NATS connect does not set tls.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to connect to a NATS cluster with a self-signed cert and ships, exposing the messaging bus to MITM.
